items.title: Prompty: Arbitrary code execution via JavaScript frontmatter in TypeScript loader
items.description:
## Summary
The TypeScript Prompty loader used `gray-matter` without overriding executable frontmatter engines. `gray-matter` supports JavaScript frontmatter blocks such as `---js` and evaluates them while parsing. An attacker-controlled `.prompty` file could therefore execute arbitrary JavaScript during prompt loading.

## Affected package
- npm `@prompty/core` v2 prerelease line: `>= 2.0.0-alpha.1 < 2.0.0-beta.3`
- Fixed in `@prompty/core@2.0.0-beta.3`

The legacy v1 JavaScript runtime had a historical hardening change for this issue. During the v2 TypeScript runtime rebuild, the loader again called `gray-matter` directly and the vulnerable behavior was present in the v2 prerelease packages until `2.0.0-beta.3`.

## Impact
Applications that load untrusted `.prompty` files, user-provided prompt paths, or prompt bundles from less-trusted locations could execute arbitrary JavaScript in the host Node.js process during frontmatter parsing.

## Remediation
Upgrade `@prompty/core` to `2.0.0-

items.title: 15-Year-Old NGINX Vulnerability Lets Attackers Crash Workers and Achieve Remote Code Execution
items.description:
A newly disclosed flaw tracked as CVE-2026-42533 affects nginx’s script engine and has been silently exploitable since March 2011, when the map directive gained regex support. Security researcher Stan Shaw reported the bug to F5 SIRT, which coordinated a fix released in nginx 1.30.4 (stable) and 1.31.3 (mainline), along with corresponding patches for NGINX Plus […]

items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-16133 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T07:02:10Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-16133", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Low fastifyreply-from vulnerable to cross-upstream request routing via URL cache
id: 7db240d8-7e31-572a-a73e-cb9bfcd67532
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-16083 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-16083
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/20
modified: 2026/07/20
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-16083 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T07:02:10Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-16083", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-16083 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6809771; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-20, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Single source with minimal corroborating evidence
items.recommended_sla_action: MONITOR — Assess in next vulnerability review cycle
items.apex_ai_summary: [P4] CVE / Vulnerability - low severity (Risk 0.0/10). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: Exploit Public-Facing Application, Command and Scripting Interpreter (2 techniques). Actor attribution: CDB-UNATTR-CVE. AI confidence: LOW (23%) - limited source data available.

    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-16095 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-20T07:02:10Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-16095", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-16095 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:8479757; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-20, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.recommended_sla_action: MONITOR — Assess in next vulnerability review cycle
items.apex_ai_summary: [P4] CVE / Vulnerability - low severity (Risk 0.0/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. Actor attribution: CDB-UNATTR-CVE. AI confidence: LOW (20%) - limited source data available.
